methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $30M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Ideon Technologies is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Gary Agnew that has raised $30M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 9 private companies and 1 public company (LNZA) operating in the same sector and stage. The peer set includes Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita ($120M).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy base of $30M and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $57M. Peer-median analysis across the five sampled Series B climate companies yields a median valuation of $310M, with a range spanning $120M to $500M. The median peer valuation substantially exceeds the stage-progression result, reflecting the capital intensity and investor appetite characterizing the frontier-energy segment.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $48M (low) to $356M (high), with a median of $184M. The 7.4x spread between low and high reflects material dispersion in early-stage climate-tech valuations and uncertainty around Ideon's specific technology differentiation, go-to-market traction, and capital efficiency relative to named peers. Stage and capital raised alone provide insufficient precision; resolution requires assessment of team pedigree, technology validation milestones, and unit economics relative to the peer cohort.
